avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0)
avoid.reason: Implied ~81% no cuts is internally consistent with sister market (~19% any cut) but no independent July-2026 FOMC/dot-plot or inflation-path source available under no-retrieval constraint; edge not verifiable.

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027? (KXRATECUT-26DEC31)
avoid.reason: Low implied P(YES)~19% aligns with zero-cut contract; cannot ground a directional edge without current 2026 labor/CPI trajectory and Fed communication.

avoid.market: Will there be a recession in 2027? (KXRECSSNBER-27)
avoid.reason: Wide long-dated binary (mid 42.5%); definition is two consecutive negative GDP quarters spanning late-2026/2027—too much path uncertainty and no specific BEA/NBER-consistent forecast source to claim ≥8% macro edge.
Only T3.5 clears $50k volume; market ladder prices a ~3.3–3.4% print. Without the actual June/July 2026 CPI release trail or BLS nowcast under no-retrieval rules, any edge would be fabricated.
Advance estimate due ~2026-07-30; only T2.5 clears volume filter. No Atlanta Fed GDPNow / BEA tracking print available in-session to justify ≥8% edge.
All fail volume filter (<$50k) and/or require live spot and session path; no fabricated price source permitted.

notes: Regime read from book structure only: markets price sticky mid-3% inflation, low odds of 2026 cuts (~19%), moderate ~2% Q2 growth, and ~42% recession risk for 2027—consistent with neutral/higher-for-longer, not a clear inflationary spike or recession print. Strict filters left only five names (two Fed cut, recession 2027, CPI YoY>3.5%, GDP Q2>2.5%). Per rules, no trade without a credible specific source; retrieval is prohibited and sources must not be fabricated. Therefore zero trades. Re-scan when BLS CPI, BEA GDP Advance, or FOMC materials are in the supplied context.
